sections.heading: 0. Frame
sections.body: Goal: lume installable on a repo that is NOT this one, as a Claude plugin, so a non-author can run it and give feedback. The objective fixes the why (external use n>1 is the first gate on the long-off autonomy chain), the bounds (mechanical packaging only - no engine expansion, no review queue, no measurement, no autonomy), and the non-negotiables (stdlib-only, deterministic, behaviour-preserving: 227 tests + the live .lume/ stay green, wrap existing verbs rather than duplicate logic, ceremony buys its cost: no daemon/auto-upgrade). This iteration writes NO engine code; it maps how plugins are really structured, audits lume against that, and hands planning a set of forks each with a recommended lean. The verb catalog (lume verbs --json) + --json-everywhere core means plugin commands are a WRAPPER over existing verbs, not a rewrite - confirmed below.

sections.heading: 1. How a Claude plugin is actually structured (authoritative)
sections.body:
Verified against code.claude.com/docs (plugins, plugins-reference, plugin-marketplaces).
MANIFEST: .claude-plugin/plugin.json is the ONLY file under .claude-plugin/. Sole required field: name (kebab-case, used to namespace skills). Optional: version (if omitted, the git commit SHA is the version - so every commit is a new version; set it to pin releases), description, displayName, author{name,email,url}, homepage, repository, license, keywords, defaultEnabled, plus explicit component paths (skills, commands, agents, hooks, mcpServers, etc.) when not using the default locations.
LAYOUT: all components live at the PLUGIN ROOT, not inside .claude-plugin/. Auto-discovered: skills/<name>/SKILL.md (-> /plugin-name:skill), commands/*.md (legacy flat skills), agents/*.md, hooks/hooks.json, .mcp.json. KEY for us: a bin/ directory at the plugin root is automatically added to the Bash tool's PATH while the plugin is enabled - any executable there is callable by bare name.
PATHS: ${CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT} resolves to the plugin's absolute install dir (use in skills/hooks/commands to reference bundled files). ${CLAUDE_PLUGIN_DATA} (~/.claude/plugins/data/{id}/) persists across updates - NOT needed by us (lume state lives in the operator's repo .lume/, not in plugin data).
INSTALL: plugins are distributed via a marketplace.json in a git repo. User runs `/plugin marketplace add <git-url>` then `/plugin install <name>@<marketplace>` (or the `claude plugin ...` CLI equivalents). Local dev: `claude --plugin-dir ./path`. Installed plugins are copied to ~/.claude/plugins/cache/; a plugin cannot reference files outside its own dir.
CONSTRAINTS that fit lume: plugins ship arbitrary files, can run bundled scripts, and need no runtime deps if the script is stdlib-only. There is NO auto-install of Python packages - which is fine because lume is stdlib-only by mandate; the only host requirement is a python3 on PATH.

sections.heading: 2. Current lume layout, audited against that map
sections.body:
Repo root today: .lume/ (engine/ + workstreams/), docs/ (constraints,questions,scope,vision), .claude/, .gitignore (only __pycache__). NO root README, NO package metadata (no pyproject.toml/setup), NO .claude-plugin/, NO bin on PATH.
ENGINE: lives at .lume/engine/. Package is .lume/engine/lume/ (frontmatter, clock, iteration, workstream, repository, validate, migrate, seed, schemas/, cli/). Entry point .lume/engine/bin/lume is a 478-byte shim: it inserts its parent.parent (.lume/engine) on sys.path and calls lume.cli.main(sys.argv). So the engine is invoked TODAY only by relative path (.lume/engine/bin/lume ...); `lume` does not resolve as a bare command anywhere.
STATE DISCOVERY: Repository.find_lume_dir() walks cwd + parents looking for a .lume/ directory (repository.py:40). This is the load-bearing seam for packaging: the engine code and the state directory are already decoupled - the engine finds whatever .lume/ is above cwd. Nothing in the engine assumes it lives inside that .lume/.
VERB SURFACE confirmed wrappable: `lume verbs --json` emits a self-describing catalog (name/summary/args/inputs with flags+descriptions); --json is on every verb; errors are structured. A plugin command is therefore a thin shell that calls the bundled lume binary and relays --json output - no logic duplicated into plugin glue.
GAP LIST (what install requires that does not exist): (a) a real entry point so `lume` resolves without a relative path; (b) a .claude-plugin/plugin.json exposing verbs as commands; (c) a root README orienting a new operator (what lume is, install, the iteration loop, first command); (d) `lume seed` reachable as the new operator's first step on their own repo; (e) a distribution/marketplace entry + a documented one-line install that does NOT auto-upgrade.

sections.heading: 3. The coexistence model (the subtle requirement)
sections.body:
Because find_lume_dir() walks UP from cwd for a .lume/ dir, the engine's CODE location is independent of the operator's STATE location. That gives the clean separation packaging needs:
- Installed on a stranger's repo: the engine lives at ${CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT} (in ~/.claude/plugins/cache/), exposed as a `lume` executable on PATH via the plugin's bin/. The operator's state is a .lume/ created in THEIR repo by `lume seed`. The engine never copies itself into their .lume/.
- In THIS repo (lume-developing-lume): the engine still lives at .lume/engine/ and is run by .lume/engine/bin/lume against this repo's own .lume/workstreams/. If the plugin is ALSO installed here, two `lume` paths could exist (the in-repo shim and the PATH binary) but both resolve the SAME state via find_lume_dir(), so behaviour is preserved; the source of truth for our own dogfooding stays .lume/engine/. Planning must decide whether the engine PHYSICALLY MOVES out of .lume/engine (cleaner package, but disrupts dogfooding paths and history) or is EXPOSED IN PLACE / mirrored (preserves the 227 tests and our own loop untouched). This is fork F3 below and the single biggest design call of the workstream.
- ${CLAUDE_PLUGIN_DATA} is deliberately unused: lume state belongs in the operator's repo under version-controllable .lume/, not in a hidden per-plugin data dir.

sections.heading: 4. Packaging mechanism options (stdlib-only tension)
sections.body:
The objective flags the tension: a pyproject.toml + console_script entry is the idiomatic 'lume resolves on PATH' answer, but it implies a pip install step and packaging deps, which sits awkwardly with stdlib-only and 'install must itself be simple'. Plugins give us a SECOND, lighter path that avoids pip entirely: drop the executable in the plugin's bin/ (auto-added to PATH while enabled). Options for planning:
A) Plugin bin/ shim only (no pip). bin/lume is the existing stdlib shim, adjusted to find the bundled package via ${CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT} or its own __file__. Zero runtime deps, zero pip, install == plugin install. Lightest; matches 'no ceremony'. Host needs python3 on PATH.
B) pyproject.toml + console_script, installed via pipx/pip, plugin commands just call `lume`. Idiomatic for non-plugin CLI use too, but adds a pip dependency and a second install path; 'stdlib-only' is about RUNTIME deps (still satisfiable) but the build/install gains a packaging toolchain.
C) zipapp/shim (stdlib `zipapp`) producing a single-file `lume.pyz`. Stdlib-only, single artifact, but more moving parts than (A) for little gain given bin/ already works.
LEAN: A (plugin bin/ shim) as the plugin install path, because the plugin runtime already puts bin/ on PATH and it adds no deps; keep the door open to also publishing B later for users who want lume outside Claude Code, but B is NOT required by done-when. Decide in planning.

sections.heading: 5. Command surface: thin shells over verbs
sections.body: Because lume verbs --json is self-describing and --json is universal, plugin commands map 1:1 onto verbs. Two sub-questions for planning (fork F4): (i) granularity - one command per verb (lume:status, lume:open, lume:seed, ...) vs a small curated set covering the loop (seed -> status -> open -> approve/start -> handback -> accept) plus a catch-all; (ii) richness - a thin shell that runs `lume <verb> --json` and relays, vs a richer skill that interprets output and guides the operator through the loop. The objective leans 'thin shells, wrapper not rewrite'. A SKILL.md per loop-step (model-invocable, namespaced) likely beats raw commands/*.md because the new operator wants guidance ('you are at handback; next is accept'), and the skill body can call the bundled binary by bare name. README is partly hand-written (what/why/install/first command) and partly derivable from the verb catalog (the command list) - decide how much to generate vs write (fork F6).

sections.heading: 6. Scope held / deferred
sections.body: NO engine code this iteration; artifact is discovery.json only. Explicitly OUT (later workstreams in the chain): the awaiting-my-review queue, nesting, value/cost measurement, reputation, any autonomy. Also OUT of this workstream: copying mcp-cli's self-driving pipeline / event bus / daemon - install is a one-time operator-run action. Deferred to PLANNING (iteration 2): resolve forks F1-F6 below and slice execution, with the engine-move-vs-expose-in-place decision (F3) sized first because it gates everything and carries the largest blast radius for the 227 tests and our own dogfooding.

sections.heading: 7. Open forks for planning
sections.body:
F1 packaging mechanism: (A) plugin bin/ shim only [lean], (B) pyproject+console_script via pip, (C) stdlib zipapp .pyz. Pick the plugin install path; decide if a non-plugin path ships now or later.
F2 manifest shape: minimal (name only) vs fuller (name+version+description+author+repository+keywords); set version explicitly or ride the git-SHA-as-version default. Lean: fuller manifest, explicit version pinned to lume's 0.1.0 line.
F3 engine location (BIGGEST): physically MOVE the engine out of .lume/engine into a plugin-root package, vs EXPOSE IN PLACE / mirror so .lume/engine and the 227 tests and our own dogfooding paths stay untouched. Must stay behaviour-preserving. Size this first.
F4 command surface: per-verb commands vs curated loop-step skills; thin relay vs guiding skill. Lean: a small set of loop-step SKILL.md wrappers calling the bundled `lume` by bare name.
F5 install UX + no-auto-upgrade: the exact one-line install (marketplace add + plugin install, or --plugin-dir for dev), how seed becomes the first step on the operator's repo, and how updates stay explicit (no daemon, no background upgrade).
F6 README authorship: how much is hand-written (what/why/install/iteration loop/first command) vs generated from the verb catalog; where it lives (repo root) and how it orients a stranger to their first iteration.
F7 phasing: recommend (a) resolve F3 + stand up the plugin skeleton (.claude-plugin/plugin.json + bin entry that resolves `lume`), then (b) command/skill wrappers over verbs, then (c) root README + install path + a clean-repo install smoke test proving `lume seed` works on a fresh repo. Keep each slice behaviour-preserving with the test suite green.
